insights:
The single best deal among current models is DeepSeek’s V4 Flash. With an intelligence score of 40 out of 60, a blended price of just $0.060 per million tokens, and a value score of 666.7—more than 50% higher than the next competitor—it offers an exceptional balance of capability and cost. It delivers 110 tokens per second throughput and supports a 1,000K context window, making it suitable for long-document tasks and high-volume production workloads. No other model at or near its price point provides comparable intelligence per dollar.

Notable runners-up include MiMo-V2-Flash, which matches the $0.060 price but achieves only 25/60 intelligence and a 256K context, making it a weaker option for nuanced reasoning. InclusionAI’s Ling-2.6-flash also costs the same but scores just 14/60 on intelligence, though it outputs 166 tokens per second—fastest among the low-cost group. For teams willing to spend more for higher intelligence, DeepSeek V4 Pro ($0.18, 44/60 intelligence) and MiniMax M3 ($0.22, 44/60 intelligence with a 1501 code ELO) represent solid upgrades, but their value scores fall to 244.4 and 200.0 respectively. MiMo-V2.5-Pro ($0.18, 42/60) is another coding-focused alternative, albeit slower at 46 t/s.

For the most common cost-sensitive API use case—general conversational or text-generation workloads—the actionable recommendation is to start with DeepSeek V4 Flash. Its intelligence is sufficient for most tasks, its price is the lowest among capable models, and its 1,000K context eliminates the need for chunking in many scenarios. If domain-specific coding accuracy is paramount, consider MiniMax M3 as a slight premium option; otherwise, V4 Flash provides the best value.
